title: Describe the interaction that actually ships
statement: UI copy must name the real gesture and outcome, especially hover versus click, rather than describe an idealized interaction.
rationale: Incorrect instructions make a working feature feel broken and invalidate documentation evidence.

prefer: Verify and name the exact shipped gesture and result.
avoid: Describing click behavior when the feature uses hover, or vice versa.
Copy based only on intended behavior.

when: Copy instructs the user how to interact with a feature.
not_when: The text is explicitly conceptual and labeled as such.

evidence.summary: Corrected feature copy to describe clicking, browser tabs, and interactive pills, while removing redundant explanation.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Candidate pending independent recurrence or explicit owner approval.

positive_examples: Verify and name the exact shipped gesture and result.
negative_examples: Describing click behavior when the feature uses hover, or vice versa.
Copy based only on intended behavior.

checks: Does the described gesture match the current rendered product?
